# pause

**Instance Method**

Pauses playback at the current time.

## Declaration

```swift
void pause();
```

## Discussion

This ensures that [doc://com.apple.livephotoskitjs/documentation/LivePhotosKitJS/LivePhotosKit.Player/wantsToPlay] is `false`, meaning that if loading activity completes, playback will not begin spuriously.

If the Player is already not playing, then, aside from maintaining the [doc://com.apple.livephotoskitjs/documentation/LivePhotosKitJS/LivePhotosKit.Player/wantsToPlay] value, this does nothing.

To resume playback, call the [doc://com.apple.livephotoskitjs/documentation/LivePhotosKitJS/LivePhotosKit.Player/play] or [doc://com.apple.livephotoskitjs/documentation/LivePhotosKitJS/LivePhotosKit.Player/toggle] method.

## Instance Methods

- **beginFinishingPlaybackEarly**: Clips the duration of the renderer early so that the animation begins to fade back to the photo component earlier than it would ordinarily.
- **play**: Starts playback if the player is ready, or resumes playback if the player is paused.
- **stop**: Stops playback and rewinds to the current time of zero.
- **toggle**: Starts playing the video component of Live Photo if the video is paused, or pauses the video if it is playing.
- **updateSize**: Updates the size of the player.
